content.problemStatement:
Given two arrays AAA and BBB, each of size NNN, where each array consists of **distinct** elements.

Find the number of elements that are common in both the arrays.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the size of both the arrays.
The second line contains NNN space separated integers - the elements of array AAA.
The third line contains NNN space separated integers - the elements of array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of elements that are common in both the arrays.
constraints: All elements inside each array are distinct
Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2*10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains integer N, the size of both arrays. The second line contains N space‑separated distinct integers – the elements of array A. The third line contains N space‑separated distinct integers – the elements of array B.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of elements that appear in both arrays.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: common elements are 1, 3 and 4 → 3.\nTest case 2: common elements are 1, 2 and 4 → 3.\nTest case 3: no common element → 0.
The two arrays share no element, so the answer is 0.
All five numbers appear in both arrays, therefore the answer is 5.

solution.approach: For each test case store all elements of the first array in a hash set (or unordered_set). Then iterate over the second array and increase a counter for every element that is found in the set. Because look‑ups in a hash set are O(1) on average, the whole procedure runs in linear time. The set is cleared for the next test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the hash set of the first array.
